P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
Technical Assistance 
(916) 322-5662 
Administration 
Executive/legal 
322-5901 
Enforcement 
322-64.41 
Statements of Ec.,nomic Interest 
322-6444 
322-5660 
Mr. Fletcher H. Swan 
1224 Orange Grove Avenue 
So0th Pasadena, CA 
91030 
Dear Mr. Swan: 
Your letter asks: 
April 21, 1983 
Re: 
Your Letter Dated April 7, 
1983, Our No. A-83-098 
Under what circumstances maya member of a City 
Council and/or a Community Redevelopment Agency sell 
services and material to the City or Agency involved? 
Under the Political Reform Actl/ the Fair Political 
Practices Commission is charged with advising public officials 
who have duties under the Act. 
Section 83114. 
Your letter does 
not indicate whether you are a public official. 
In addition, 
our advice is limited to the official who requests it; we do not 
advise third parties regarding the duties and obligations of 
others who are officials. 
Section 83114. 
However, we can provide you with general information about 
the requirements of the Political Reform Act. 
We enclose a copy 
of our pamphlet and of the Act itself for your review. 
Generally, the conflict of interest provisions of the Act 
prohibit a public official from making, participating in making, 
or using his or her official position to influence a govern-
mental decision in which he or she has a financial interest. 
Section 87100. 
What constitutes a financial interest is defined 
in Section 87103. 
l/ Government Code Sections 81000-91014, hereinafter the 
"Act". 
All statutory references are to the Government Code 
unless otherwise specified. 


Mr. Fletcher H. Swan 
April 21, 1983 
Page Two 
Your question also suggests the possible applicability of 
Government Code Sections 1090, et~. These provisions are not 
a part of the Political Reform Act and, therefore, are not 
administered or interpreted by the Fair Political Practices 
Commission. 
You may wish to seek advice from the Attorney 
General regarding these provisions. 
This may be accomplished 
through your local county counsel's office or through your 
legislative representatives. 
REL: km 
Enclosures 
Sincerely,

sections.question: Under what circumstances may a member of a City Council and/or a Community Redevelopment Agency sell services and material to the City or Agency involved?
sections.conclusion: Generally, the conflict of interest provisions of the Act prohibit a public official from making, participating in making, or using his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in which he or she has a financial interest. Section 87100. What constitutes a financial interest is defined in Section 87103. The FPPC also notes that provisions under Government Code Sections 1090, et seq. are not administered by the FPPC and the requester may seek advice from the Attorney General regarding those provisions.
sections.question_synthetic: The requester asks about the circumstances under which a City Council member or Community Redevelopment Agency member may sell services and materials to their respective government entities.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC explains that under Section 87100 of the Political Reform Act, public officials are generally prohibited from using their official position to influence governmental decisions in which they have a financial interest, with financial interest defined in Section 87103. The FPPC declines to provide specific advice since the requester did not indicate whether they are a public official, but notes that separate provisions under Government Code Sections 1090 et seq. may also apply and should be addressed through the Attorney General.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document clearly identifies question and conclusion sections. Requester (Fletcher H. Swan) is not identified as a public official, so FPPC limits advice to general information. Document references Political Reform Act Section 83114 regarding FPPC's advisory role restrictions.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Under what circumstances may a member of a City Council and/or a Community Redevelopment Agency sell services and material to the City or Agency involved?

CONCLUSION: Generally, the conflict of interest provisions of the Act prohibit a public official from making, participating in making, or using his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in which he or she has a financial interest. Section 87100. What constitutes a financial interest is defined in Section 87103. The FPPC also notes that provisions under Government Code Sections 1090, et seq. are not administered by the FPPC and the requester may seek advice from the Attorney General regarding those provisions.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responds to a query about whether City Council members or CRA members may sell services and materials to their government entities. While declining to provide specific advice due to uncertainty about the requester's official status, the FPPC explains that the Political Reform Act's conflict of interest provisions prohibit officials from using their position to influence decisions in which they have financial interests, and notes that additional provisions under Government Code Sections 1090 et seq. may also apply.
